response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and distributed-systems engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js credentials, but the role's core requirement is deep, sustained C++ expertise in an industrial automation context. His only documented C++ work is a roughly six-month engagement at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor-thesis acoustic project; his high-performance systems experience is in DLang, not C++. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IoT protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), IEC 62443 secure-development lifecycle practices, or on-premises embedded/platform development — all of which are central to this position.
strengths: Demonstrated ability to build and operate high-throughput distributed systems handling millions of requests per second (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Node.js and React experience aligns with the JD's advantageous skill set
Strong agile and software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring)
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — approximately six months at NI in 2014–2015 and one academic thesis project; the sustained career stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang
No industrial automation, IoT, or embedded-systems background; no exposure to required protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
No evidence of Secure Development Lifecycle implementation, IEC 62443 compliance, or any industrial cybersecurity work

key_factors.factor: No industrial/IoT domain or protocol experience
High-throughput distributed systems and event-driven architecture background
